evals.prompt: I want to build a barcode scanning web app from scratch using BarcodeCapture. I don't have any existing code yet.
evals.expected_output: The skill offers the pre-built sample as the starting point, asks whether the user is using React or vanilla JS, provides the relevant sample URL, and guides them through cloning it and replacing the license key and symbologies.

assertions.text: The pre-built sample is offered before any manual integration code is shown
The vanilla JS sample URL (BarcodeCaptureSimpleSample) or the React sample URL (BarcodeCaptureReactSample) is mentioned
The user is asked whether they are using React or plain JS/TypeScript (or a similar framework question)
Replacing the license key placeholder is mentioned
Adjusting symbologies is mentioned

evals.prompt: I want to add BarcodeCapture to my web app. Here's my main file: empty-app.ts. I need to scan EAN-13 and Code 128 barcodes for a retail app.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md, writes complete BarcodeCapture integration code into empty-app.ts, and shows the setup checklist.

assertions.text: Setup checklist is shown (mentions @scandit/web-datacapture-core and @scandit/web-datacapture-barcode npm packages)
DataCaptureContext.forLicenseKey( is present with libraryLocation and moduleLoaders
barcodeCaptureLoader() is present in moduleLoaders
License key placeholder (e.g. ENTER YOUR SCANDIT LICENSE KEY or similar) is present
BarcodeCaptureSettings is created with new BarcodeCaptureSettings()
enableSymbologies is called on settings
Symbology.EAN13UPCA is enabled
Symbology.Code128 is enabled
No symbologies except EAN13UPCA and Code128 are enabled
Camera.pickBestGuess() is called
BarcodeCapture.recommendedCameraSettings is used
BarcodeCapture.forContext( is present and awaited
addListener with didScan callback is present
session.newlyRecognizedBarcode is accessed inside didScan
await barcodeCapture.setEnabled(false) is called inside didScan before processing the scan result
DataCaptureView.forContext( is present and awaited
view.connectToElement( is called
BarcodeCaptureOverlay.withBarcodeCaptureForView( is present and awaited
switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.On) is called to start the camera
The barcodeCaptureListener is stored in a named variable so it can be removed
removeListener is called on the barcodeCapture instance in cleanup (unmount or equivalent)
switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.Off) is called in cleanup

evals.prompt: I have a basic React app and need to integrate BarcodeCapture barcode scanning. I want to scan EAN-13 and Code 128. Here's my component: empty-react-app.tsx.
evals.expected_output: The skill integrates BarcodeCapture into the React component using a useRef for the capture element, useEffect for initialization and cleanup, and proper async patterns.

assertions.text: useRef is used to reference the capture DOM element
useEffect is used for initialization
DataCaptureContext.forLicenseKey( is present with moduleLoaders and barcodeCaptureLoader()
License key placeholder is present
BarcodeCaptureSettings is created and enableSymbologies is called
Symbology.EAN13UPCA and Symbology.Code128 are enabled
BarcodeCapture.forContext( is present
addListener with didScan callback is present
view.connectToElement is called with the ref element
switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.Off) is called in the useEffect cleanup return
removeListener is called on the barcodeCapture instance in the useEffect cleanup return
Setup checklist mentions @scandit/web-datacapture-core and @scandit/web-datacapture-barcode npm packages
The capture element div has position: fixed or position: absolute set with non-zero width and height

evals.prompt: I want to filter duplicate scans in my BarcodeCapture app. Suppress the same barcode from being reported again for 1 second after it's first scanned.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets codeDuplicateFilter to 1000 on BarcodeCaptureSettings and preserves the existing integration.

assertions.text: settings.codeDuplicateFilter = 1000 or equivalent (a number, not TimeInterval)
The codeDuplicateFilter is set on BarcodeCaptureSettings before BarcodeCapture.forContext is called, OR applySettings is used
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, BarcodeCapture, listener, view, mount/unmount) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want to show the image of the scanned barcode in my app. How do I capture the frame when a barcode is scanned?
evals.expected_output: The skill updates didScan to include the frameData parameter and uses frameData.toBlob('image/jpeg', 0.3) without awaiting it, using .then()/.catch() to handle the result. SymbologyDescription is used for a readable symbology name.

assertions.text: frameData is added as the third parameter of didScan
frameData.toBlob("image/jpeg", 0.3) is called (or equivalent JPEG blob conversion)
toBlob is NOT awaited — .then()/.catch() is used instead
A .catch() fallback handles the case where blob conversion fails
FrameData is imported from @scandit/web-datacapture-core
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, BarcodeCapture, DataCaptureView, mount/unmount) is preserved

evals.prompt: When a barcode is scanned in my app, I want to reject it if the data starts with '00' and disable the overlay highlight for those barcodes. All other barcodes should be accepted.
evals.expected_output: The skill updates the didScan listener to check barcode.data and call overlay.setBrush(Brush.transparent) for rejected codes, preserving the existing integration.

assertions.text: barcode.data check for codes starting with '00' is present (startsWith or equivalent)
Brush.transparent or equivalent transparent brush is set on the overlay for rejected barcodes
The overlay reference is available to the listener (either stored as a variable or passed in)
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, BarcodeCapture, DataCaptureView, mount/unmount) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want to change the scan feedback in my BarcodeCapture app: vibrate on a successful scan but do NOT play the beep sound.
evals.expected_output: The skill builds a BarcodeCaptureFeedback whose success Feedback has the default vibration and a null sound, then applies it via setFeedback, preserving the existing integration.

assertions.text: BarcodeCaptureFeedback.default is used to obtain the starting feedback
feedback.success is assigned a new Feedback( instance
new Feedback( is called with Vibration.defaultVibration as the vibration argument and null as the sound argument
await barcodeCapture.setFeedback( is called to apply the feedback
The feedback is NOT applied by assigning to the read-only barcodeCapture.feedback property (semantic)
Feedback and Vibration are imported from @scandit/web-datacapture-core
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, BarcodeCapture, DataCaptureView, mount/unmount) is preserved

evals.prompt: Add a rectangular viewfinder to my BarcodeCapture scanner so users see a guide rectangle. Use the square style with a light line.
evals.expected_output: The skill creates a RectangularViewfinder with Square style and Light line style and attaches it to the overlay using the overlay's setViewfinder method, keeping a reference to the overlay.

assertions.text: new RectangularViewfinder( is created
RectangularViewfinderStyle.Square is passed as the style
RectangularViewfinderLineStyle.Light is passed as the line style
await overlay.setViewfinder( is called to attach the viewfinder
The viewfinder is attached via the overlay setViewfinder method, NOT a brush property (semantic)
RectangularViewfinder, RectangularViewfinderStyle, and RectangularViewfinderLineStyle are imported from @scandit/web-datacapture-core
The BarcodeCaptureOverlay returned by withBarcodeCaptureForView is stored in a variable so setViewfinder can be called on it
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, BarcodeCapture, DataCaptureView, mount/unmount) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want an aimer-style viewfinder (a crosshair aim point) on my BarcodeCapture preview instead of a rectangle.
evals.expected_output: The skill creates an AimerViewfinder and attaches it via overlay.setViewfinder, preserving the existing integration.

assertions.text: new AimerViewfinder( is created
await overlay.setViewfinder( is called with the AimerViewfinder
AimerViewfinder is imported from @scandit/web-datacapture-core
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, BarcodeCapture, DataCaptureView, mount/unmount) is preserved

evals.prompt: Show a laserline viewfinder (a horizontal line) over the camera preview in my BarcodeCapture app.
evals.expected_output: The skill creates a LaserlineViewfinder and attaches it via overlay.setViewfinder, preserving the existing integration.

assertions.text: new LaserlineViewfinder( is created
await overlay.setViewfinder( is called with the LaserlineViewfinder
LaserlineViewfinder is imported from @scandit/web-datacapture-core
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, BarcodeCapture, DataCaptureView, mount/unmount) is preserved

evals.prompt: Restrict my BarcodeCapture scanner to only read barcodes inside a rectangular area covering about 90% of the width and 30% of the height of the preview.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets a RectangularLocationSelection sized with Fraction units on BarcodeCaptureSettings.locationSelection, preserving the existing integration.

assertions.text: settings.locationSelection is assigned
RectangularLocationSelection.withSize( is used to build the location selection
new SizeWithUnit( is used to express the area size
MeasureUnit.Fraction is used for the size units
The locationSelection is set on BarcodeCaptureSettings before BarcodeCapture.forContext is called, OR applySettings is used afterward
RectangularLocationSelection, SizeWithUnit, and MeasureUnit are imported from @scandit/web-datacapture-core
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, BarcodeCapture, DataCaptureView, mount/unmount) is preserved

evals.prompt: Enable the full-ASCII extension for Code 39 in my BarcodeCapture app so it decodes lowercase and control characters.
evals.expected_output: The skill obtains the Code 39 SymbologySettings via settingsForSymbology and calls setExtensionEnabled('full_ascii', true), preserving the existing integration.

assertions.text: settings.settingsForSymbology(Symbology.Code39) is called to get the SymbologySettings
setExtensionEnabled is called with the extension name and true
The extension argument is the string full_ascii
Symbology.Code39 is enabled on the settings
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, BarcodeCapture, DataCaptureView, mount/unmount) is preserved

evals.prompt: Require a Mod 43 checksum for Code 39 barcodes in my BarcodeCapture scanner.
evals.expected_output: The skill gets the Code 39 SymbologySettings via settingsForSymbology and sets checksums to a Set containing Checksum.Mod43, preserving the existing integration.

assertions.text: settings.settingsForSymbology(Symbology.Code39) is called to get the SymbologySettings
The checksums property is assigned a new Set( containing Checksum.Mod43
Checksum.Mod43 is used
Checksum is imported from @scandit/web-datacapture-barcode
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, BarcodeCapture, DataCaptureView, mount/unmount) is preserved

evals.prompt: I am scanning ITF-14 codes, which are always 14 characters. Restrict the active symbol count for Interleaved 2 of 5 to 14 in my BarcodeCapture app.
evals.expected_output: The skill gets the Interleaved 2 of 5 SymbologySettings via settingsForSymbology and sets activeSymbolCounts to a number array containing 14, preserving the existing integration.

assertions.text: settings.settingsForSymbology(Symbology.InterleavedTwoOfFive) is called to get the SymbologySettings
activeSymbolCounts is assigned an array containing 14
activeSymbolCounts is assigned a number array (e.g. [14]), not a Set (semantic)
Symbology.InterleavedTwoOfFive is enabled on the settings
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, BarcodeCapture, DataCaptureView, mount/unmount) is preserved

evals.prompt: Some of my Data Matrix labels are printed bright-on-dark (color inverted). Make my BarcodeCapture app read those too.
evals.expected_output: The skill gets the Data Matrix SymbologySettings via settingsForSymbology and sets isColorInvertedEnabled to true, keeping the symbology enabled, preserving the existing integration.

assertions.text: settings.settingsForSymbology(Symbology.DataMatrix) is called to get the SymbologySettings
isColorInvertedEnabled is set to true
The property used is isColorInvertedEnabled, NOT colorInvertedEnabled (semantic)
Symbology.DataMatrix is enabled on the settings
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, BarcodeCapture, DataCaptureView, mount/unmount) is preserved

evals.prompt: I need to scan GS1 composite codes (a linear code with a 2D companion) in my BarcodeCapture app. How do I enable composite codes?
evals.expected_output: The skill enables the composite types via enabledCompositeTypes and enables the underlying symbologies via enableSymbologiesForCompositeTypes, preserving the existing integration.

assertions.text: settings.enabledCompositeTypes is assigned an array of CompositeType values
CompositeType.A is included
CompositeType.B is included
CompositeType.C is included
settings.enableSymbologiesForCompositeTypes( is called with the composite types to enable the underlying symbologies
CompositeType is imported from @scandit/web-datacapture-barcode
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, BarcodeCapture, DataCaptureView, mount/unmount) is preserved
